exercises.name: Assisted chin-up
exercises.swimDescription: Builds the vertical pulling strength used in freestyle and butterfly catches, especially when you need to hold water through the front of the pull.

exercises.name: Chest-supported row
exercises.swimDescription: Strengthens mid-back retraction for freestyle and backstroke, helping you finish the pull without shrugging or overusing the neck.

exercises.name: Straight-arm pulldown
guideCues: Keep ribs down and torso still
exercises.swimDescription: Closely matches the freestyle and butterfly catch-to-press motion, teaching the lats to press water back while the core stays still.

exercises.name: Rear delt fly
exercises.swimDescription: Supports shoulder balance for freestyle and backstroke recovery so the front of the shoulder is not doing all the work.

exercises.name: Leg press
guideCues: Drive through mid-foot, no knee lockout
exercises.swimDescription: Builds leg drive for starts, turns, and push-offs across every stroke, with the biggest transfer to wall speed and streamline distance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.swimDescription: Strengthens hamstrings and glutes for dolphin kick and freestyle kick rhythm, while also protecting the lower back during starts and turns.

exercises.name: Dumbbell external rotation
guideCues: Use light load and strict control
exercises.swimDescription: Builds rotator cuff control for freestyle and butterfly recovery, helping keep the shoulder centred through repeated overhead strokes.

exercises.name: Weighted dead bug / hollow hold
guideCues: Reach long through arms and legs
exercises.swimDescription: Improves body-line control for streamline, freestyle rotation, and butterfly undulation without the hips or ribs leaking position.

exercises.name: Farmer's carry
guideCues: Keep grip tight and ribs down
exercises.swimDescription: Builds grip, trunk stiffness, and shoulder packing that carry over to freestyle and backstroke pull stability late in a set.

exercises.name: Calf raises
exercises.swimDescription: None. Just helps my Achilles

exercises.name: Lat pulldown
guideCues: Keep shoulders down away from ears
exercises.swimDescription: Builds the main pulling muscles for freestyle and butterfly, especially the strong early catch and acceleration under the body.

exercises.name: Single-arm dumbbell row
guideCues: Lower until shoulder blade reaches forward
exercises.swimDescription: Trains one-side pulling strength and trunk control for freestyle rotation, helping each arm connect to the lat without twisting loose.

exercises.name: Dumbbell pullover
guideCues: Move dumbbell in a smooth arc
exercises.swimDescription: Works shoulder extension and lat length for freestyle and butterfly, useful for feeling a long catch without collapsing the ribs.

exercises.name: Face pull
exercises.swimDescription: Keeps the upper back and external rotators strong for freestyle, backstroke, and butterfly recovery positions.

exercises.name: Triceps pressdown
guideCues: Press rope or bar to thighs
exercises.swimDescription: Strengthens the final push phase of freestyle and butterfly, helping finish each stroke past the hip instead of slipping early.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.swimDescription: Builds leg strength and hip mobility for breaststroke kick, starts, and turns while keeping the torso upright and controlled.

exercises.name: Hamstring curl
exercises.swimDescription: Targets knee flexion for breaststroke recovery and supports hamstring durability for starts, turns, and dolphin kick.

exercises.name: Kettlebell swing
exercises.swimDescription: Develops hip snap for starts, turns, and butterfly dolphin kick power, with the strongest transfer to explosive wall push-offs.

exercises.name: Medicine ball slam
guideCues: Brace and slam through the floor
exercises.swimDescription: Builds whole-body power for butterfly and freestyle sprinting, linking the overhead position to a fast core-driven pull down.

exercises.name: Low twist or high twist
guideCues: Stand tall with cable to one side
exercises.swimDescription: Trains rotation for freestyle and backstroke so the hips, ribs, and pull connect instead of the arm working alone.

programs.name: Flexibility
programs.trainingNote: Aim for 10-15 minutes total. Best after a shower, after a walk, or in the evening. For a stiff morning back, keep it gentle and don't force the toe-touch first thing.

exercises.name: Cat-camel
exercises.swimDescription: Gently moves the spine through flexion and extension, useful for loosening the back before land training and improving body awareness for all strokes.
guideCues: Move slowly from tailbone to neck

exercises.name: Child's pose
exercises.swimDescription: Opens the lats, shoulders, and lower back after swim training, helping freestyle and butterfly overhead reach feel less restricted.

exercises.name: Knees side-to-side / lower back rotations
exercises.swimDescription: Restores gentle trunk rotation for freestyle and backstroke while reducing lower-back stiffness from kicking, sitting, or gym work.
guideCues: Keep shoulders relaxed on the floor
Let knees move side to side
Keep the range easy and controlled

exercises.name: Knee-to-chest stretch
exercises.swimDescription: Relieves lower-back and glute tightness so streamline, turns, and body position feel easier across all strokes.

exercises.name: Hip flexor stretch
exercises.swimDescription: Opens the front of the hips for better streamline, flutter kick, dolphin kick, and breaststroke body position.

exercises.name: Glute / figure-four stretch
exercises.swimDescription: Improves hip rotation and glute comfort for starts, turns, and breaststroke kick setup without forcing the lower back.
guideCues: Keep the stretch in the glute

exercises.name: Hamstring stretch on chair
exercises.swimDescription: Improves posterior-chain flexibility for starts, turns, and a cleaner streamline line without aggressively forcing a toe touch.
guideCues: Hinge forward with a long back

exercises.name: Calf stretch against wall
exercises.swimDescription: Targets straight-knee and bent-knee calf mobility for stronger push-offs, better ankle position, and more relaxed flutter and dolphin kick.
guideCues: Keep heel heavy toward the floor

exercises.name: Standing forward fold with bent knees
exercises.swimDescription: Lets the back and hamstrings relax gently, supporting starts, turns, and everyday recovery without forcing morning stiffness.
guideCues: Bend knees and let back relax

exercises.name: Open-book thoracic rotations
exercises.swimDescription: Improves upper-back rotation for freestyle and backstroke, helping the shoulder move from a better rib-cage position.
guideCues: Follow the hand with your eyes
